story: Mexico Icon Guillermo Ochoa Retires, Joins Messi And Ronaldo In Rare Feat
title: When Goalkeeping Greats Ride Off into the Sunset
overlay: Goalkeeper legends hang up their gloves
script:
Guillermo Ochoa announces retirement, joining Ronaldo and Messi in their exclusive club.

A Mexican soccer icon, Ochoa's decision marks the end of an illustrious career spanning over two decades. 
His impressive longevity is a testament to his exceptional skill and unwavering dedication to the sport. 
Now, he joins a rare fraternity of players who've managed to leave an indelible mark on the game. 
Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi are among those who've achieved similar feats, with Ronaldo famously splashing around in an underground pool with his fiancée Georgina. 
Ronaldo's retirement has been ominously predicted to bring 'tragedy' to Portugal. 
As the soccer world bid farewell to Ochoa, a new generation of stars is rising, like Lamine Yamal and Erling Haaland. 
Can anyone else join this elite group, and what does the future hold for soccer's greatest legends?
